name: CDC Environmental Public Health Tracking Network
agency: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
coverage_notes: National anchor source, but radon metric availability varies by state and API endpoint.

name: New York State Environmental Public Health Tracking Radon Export
agency: New York State Department of Health
coverage_notes: Submitted tests are not a statistically designed survey; preserve that caveat in page copy.

name: Minnesota Public Health Data Access Radon Data
coverage_notes: Strong county and tract coverage; lab-reported testing behavior can bias results.

name: Virginia Radon Test Results
coverage_notes: VDH says aggregate data used for the map include number of tests, maximum result, and average result by locality for 2016-2024. Tableau Public summary data, crosstab, and workbook export are permission-denied, so RadonVerdict normalizes the rendered official table and preserves VDH's voluntary-reporting and sub-25-test suppression caveats.

name: Pennsylvania DEP Radon Test Data by ZIP Code
coverage_notes: DEP SSRS report exports one ZIP at a time. RadonVerdict caches ZIP CSV exports and rolls targeted high-intent counties to county-level basement and first-floor metrics through the local ZIP-to-primary-county map.

name: Tennessee Environmental Public Health Tracking Radon Data
coverage_notes: Official TN Health Data story embeds county average and ZIP 4.0+ ArcGIS layers. County and ZIP values are based on test kit and mitigation company data and cannot predict a specific home's result.

name: New Jersey DEP Radon Potential Map and Tier Tables
agency: New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection
coverage_notes: NJ DEP publishes radon potential tiers derived from testing data; this is a tier source rather than a county mean source.

name: Kansas Environmental Public Health Tracking Radon Data
agency: Kansas Department of Health and Environment
coverage_notes: KDHE ArcGIS Data Explorer publishes county radon summaries by year. The public program page explains that contractors and laboratories report testing and mitigation data to KDHE within 90 days.

name: Mississippi State Department of Health Indoor Radon
coverage_notes: MSDH provides official radon program context but no stable public county measurement table was found in this pass.

name: EPA/USGS Mississippi Residential Radon Survey
agency: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency and U.S. Geological Survey
coverage_notes: EPA Mississippi supporting documentation includes a county table from the 1990-1991 State/EPA Residential Radon Survey. RadonVerdict ingests Alcorn County as historical official context because no current public Mississippi county measurement table was found.

name: Missouri DHSS Residential Radon Testing in Missouri
agency: Missouri Department of Health and Senior Services
coverage_notes: MDHSS publishes a public ArcGIS residential radon dashboard. RadonVerdict ingests the county summary layer for total tests, average, and maximum, then computes 4.0+ share from non-negative point-level Final_Result records because -999 is used as a privacy/suppression sentinel.

name: Utah EPHT Radon Test Kit Results
agency: Utah Department of Health and Human Services
coverage_notes: Utah DHHS/EPHT IBIS exposes separate public query configurations for average results, count of tests, count by result category, and median/maximum. RadonVerdict submits county-grouped 2006-2019 queries and combines the outputs so retained Utah pages get average, test volume, 4.0+ share, median, and high-end values from the same official system.

name: Colorado Environmental Public Health Tracking Radon Data
agency: Colorado Department of Public Health and Environment
coverage_notes: CDPHE publishes pre-mitigation county and census-tract radon results for 2005-2024. The dataset is not a statistically designed survey of every home.

name: Illinois IEMA-OHS Licensed Radon Measurement Dashboard
agency: Illinois Emergency Management Agency - Office of Homeland Security
coverage_notes: IEMA-OHS publishes a public radon dashboard from annual reports submitted by licensed measurement and mitigation professionals. Measurement data cover calendar years 2003-2019 and use the highest submitted measurement for each address. RadonVerdict queries only county-level aggregates, filters invalid tests and negative result sentinels, and does not store address-level records.

name: Iowa HHS Radon Dashboard County Metrics
agency: Iowa Department of Health and Human Services
coverage_notes: Iowa HHS/IDPH exposes county median radon values through a public Tableau CSV export. RadonVerdict treats these as county median test-result context, not a property-level prediction.

name: North Carolina DHHS Radon Data Map
agency: North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services
coverage_notes: NCDHHS publishes the highest measured radon level in each county and warns that county map data should not be used to predict a particular building's result. RadonVerdict therefore stores this as a high-end signal only, not as an average.

name: Wisconsin Radon Program
coverage_notes: Wisconsin DHS publishes ZIP-level indoor radon test summaries for 1995-2016. RadonVerdict rolls them up to county with test-count weighting and preserves the testing-only caveat.
